info.title: Canary API
info.description: Machine-consumable contract for the Canary observability API. Canary exposes deterministic, bounded responses for error ingestion, health checks, incidents, timelines, operator management, and webhook delivery visibility.

x-agent-guide.summary: Register webhooks as wake-up hints, then treat the timeline as the durable source of truth for correctness.
flow: Create a webhook subscription for the business events that should wake the agent.
Persist the last processed timeline cursor after each successful polling cycle.
When a webhook arrives, poll GET /api/v1/timeline from the last persisted cursor.
Process the returned events in order, then persist the response cursor before going idle again.
Write incident or error-group annotations back to Canary when the agent completes follow-up work.
x-agent-guide.webhook_contract: Deduplicate deliveries by x-delivery-id. Webhooks are non-authoritative wake-up hints; the timeline is the durable event log and must be replayed for correctness.
x-agent-guide.crash_recovery: After a crash or restart, resume from the last persisted cursor, replay missed timeline events, and continue polling until the API returns no further cursor.
x-agent-guide.cursor_precedence: When both `after` and `cursor` are provided on timeline-style pagination endpoints, Canary resolves `after` first and ignores `cursor` for that request.
x-agent-guide.incident_entrypoint: On an `incident.opened` webhook, call `GET /api/v1/incidents/{id}` once — the response carries summary, the full incident metadata, up to 25 correlated signals with per-type context, up to 20 newest annotations, and up to 5 most recent timeline events. Only fall through to /query, /timeline, or /errors/{id} when `signals_truncated` or `annotations_truncated` is true, or the agent needs raw stack traces.

201.description: Check-in stored and monitor state updated.

get.description: Provide `service`, `error_class`, or `group_by=error_class`. Service-scoped queries default to `window=1h`; error-class and group-by queries default to `window=24h`.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor for grouped error pagination.

200.description: Unified report in JSON or CSV form.

get.description: If both `after` and `cursor` are supplied, `after` takes precedence.

get.summary: Page through webhook delivery ledger rows
get.description: If both `after` and `cursor` are supplied, `after` takes precedence.

get.summary: List recent checks for a target
Get incident detail with bounded signals, annotations, and timeline
get.description: One-call payload for responder agents: deterministic summary, incident metadata, up to 25 correlated signals, up to 20 newest annotations, and up to 5 most recent timeline events. Truncation is signalled by `signals_truncated` and `annotations_truncated`.

post.summary: Create an annotation for an incident

get.summary: List annotations for an error group

post.summary: Create an annotation for an error group

get.summary: List annotations for any signal subject
get.description: Returns annotations attached to the specified subject, newest-first, with cursor pagination (max 50 per page). Supports subject_type: incident, error_group, target, monitor. Responders use this to read prior coordination signal on the underlying subject (e.g. has anyone already filed a PR for this error_group?).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response. Pass to fetch the next page.

post.summary: Create an annotation on any signal subject
post.description: Responders stamp opaque metadata (PR links, ack tokens, fix references) onto an incident, error_group, target, or monitor. Canary owns the surface; consumers own the content. Fires an annotation.added webhook with signed HMAC + X-Delivery-Id ledger entry.

post.summary: Connect a service and return onboarding snippets

bearerAuth.description: Send `Authorization: Bearer sk_live_...` with an `ingest-only`, `read-only`, or `admin` API key.

UnauthorizedProblem.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked API key.

RateLimitedProblem.description: The client exceeded a per-key or per-IP rate limit.

NotFoundProblem.description: The requested resource does not exist.

PayloadTooLargeProblem.description: The request body exceeded a size limit.

ForbiddenProblem.description: API key is valid but lacks the required scope.

annotation_count.description: Count of annotations on this signal's underlying error_group subject. Responders use this to skip re-investigation when prior coordination signal already exists.
Count of annotations on this signal's underlying target/monitor subject.

incident_id.description: Populated for subject_type=incident. Legacy compatibility field.

group_hash.description: Populated for subject_type=error_group. Legacy compatibility field.

summary.description: Deterministic summary of annotations on the subject (total count + latest agent).

cursor.description: Opaque cursor for fetching the next page, or null when the current page is the last.

ApiKeyScope.description: Authorization scope attached to an API key.
